Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Resolve Destiny Pham's loan record in the LOS using mortgage_los_search_loans with a query related to "Destiny Pham" or "Pham" or "LN-2026-00610" (or similar) to discover the internal loan_id "los_loan_3662f4616df5", loan_number "LN-2026-00610", status "processing", loan_type "conventional", loan_amount 305000, assigned_processor "los_staff_c6b42763fb1f" (Elena Marchetti), assigned_lo "los_staff_930d770d3309" (Priya Desai), borrower_id "los_borrower_d679dee523c0", property_address "3369 Idlewild Rd, Charlotte, NC 28269", and rate_lock_expiration "2026-03-30" -- the prompt hands over no IDs so every subsequent LOS call depends on this lookup.
OE 2: Pull every condition on Destiny's loan using mortgage_los_get_conditions with loan_id "los_loan_3662f4616df5" (and optionally status "open") to discover the two open conditions: (a) condition_id "los_cond_pham610dep01", condition_type "prior_to_docs", issued_date "2026-03-15", description "Source of funds documentation for $14,000 cash deposit on bank statement -- signed LOX alone is insufficient; underwriter requires supporting evidence (Marketplace listings, buyer message threads, or withdrawal trail) tying the deposit to the stated furniture sales", with notes spelling out the exact UW-acceptable supporting-documentation menu ("listing screenshots, Messenger conversations with buyers, or any withdrawal/transfer trail showing proceeds moving into the account"); and (b) condition_id "los_cond_pham610lock02", condition_type "prior_to_closing", referencing that the original rate lock expired 2026-03-31. The deposit condition content directly drives OE 7 and OE 8.
OE 3: List every document currently on Destiny's file using mortgage_los_get_documents with loan_id "los_loan_3662f4616df5" to enumerate the 14 document checklist items -- received items include urla, credit_report, paystub_1, paystub_2 and additional income/asset docs received 2026-03-10, while w2_current remains status "required" -- and confirm that no document already on file independently substantiates the $14,000 Facebook Marketplace deposit narrative, so the deposit condition is not closable from existing uploads.
OE 4: Pull the internal Slack back-and-forth on this file using conversations_search_messages with a search_query related to "LN-2026-00610" or "Destiny Pham" or "Facebook Marketplace" or "$14k deposit" (or similar) in #loan-processing (channel C002) to recover the prior chatter on the file -- Camille Foster's lock-sweep flag ("Flagging LN-2026-00610 on my lock sweep. 3/23 exp and still sitting in cond approval.") and Sofia Reyes's UW-condition heads-up ("Just saw new UW cond on LN-2026-00610 for a $14k cash dep from 2 wks pre-app. Going to chase LOX/support from borrower.") -- and, critically, the Sofia -> Elena thread where Sofia asks for a gut check ("@Elena quick gut check on LN-2026-00610 -- borrower says the $14k cash dep was from selling furniture on Facebook Marketplace but she has no receipts. Have you seen UW take screenshots/chat history + LOX on something like that or is this prob dead without a paper trail?") and Elena's threaded reply ("Need more than just the letter. Best shot is FB Marketplace listing screenshots, Messenger convos, anything showing item/prices, maybe pics of the furniture and a written sale breakdown. Cash is the hard part though."). Elena's reply confirms that Marketplace listing screenshots and Messenger buyer conversations are the primary supporting evidence she and Sofia identified, while the full three-item supporting-documentation menu -- including the bank-side withdrawal/transfer trail -- is explicitly documented in the underwriter's condition notes discovered in OE 2 and further corroborated by Sofia's escalation email to Carlos in OE 5 (which specifically names "screenshots, buyer messages, withdrawal trail").
OE 5: Pull the email chain with the borrower on the deposit condition using search_emails with a query related to "LN-2026-00610" or "destiny.pham@gmail.com" or "$14,000 deposit" or "LOX" (or similar), searching both folder_name "INBOX" and folder_name "SENT" since the tool defaults to INBOX and the outbound side of this chain lives in SENT, to retrieve at least the three existing threads -- "Need LOX for $14,000 deposit on LN-2026-00610" (Sofia's initial request to the borrower, in SENT), "Re: Need LOX for $14,000 deposit on LN-2026-00610" (Destiny's one-sentence Facebook Marketplace reply, in INBOX, that the lender's underwriter has already rejected as insufficient), and "LN-2026-00610 deposit condition may stall file" (internal Sofia -> Carlos escalation, in SENT) -- so the agent does not re-request anything already received or contradict what Sofia already communicated to her.
OE 6: Resolve Destiny Pham's CRM contact record using crm_search_contacts with full_name "Destiny Pham" to discover crm_crmcontact_24b6f46325f0 with email "destiny.pham@gmail.com" and phone "(980) 727-1373" -- the phone matches the LOS borrower record, disambiguating the correct Borrower row from seven near-duplicate "Destiny Pham" CRM contact rows that share the same email but carry different phone numbers, addresses, and job titles. OE 9 requires this specific contact_id.
OE 7: Update the $14,000 deposit condition with the required supporting-document list using mortgage_los_update_condition with condition_id "los_cond_pham610dep01", status "open" (kept open -- this is not a clearance, it is documenting the required evidence set for the next processor), and notes listing the three-item supporting-documentation menu pulled from OE 2 and reconciled against the Sofia/Elena alignment in OE 4: (a) Facebook Marketplace listing screenshots for the furniture items sold, (b) Messenger buyer conversation threads tying to each listing, and (c) a bank-side withdrawal/transfer trail or cash-equivalent deposit trail showing proceeds moving into the deposit account.
OE 8: Send a fresh plain-language email to the borrower using send_email with sender "elena.marchetti@keystonemortgage.com", recipients ["destiny.pham@gmail.com"], subject relating to next steps on the $14,000 deposit documentation (e.g., "Next steps on your $14,000 deposit documentation" or similar), and content that walks Destiny through the same three-item list from OE 7 (Marketplace listing screenshots, Messenger buyer threads, and a bank withdrawal/transfer trail) plus a plain-language explanation of why the signed LOX alone was not enough for the lender's underwriter, framed so the borrower does not feel the requirements are shifting.
OE 9: Log a CRM engagement note on Destiny's contact record using crm_create_engagement with engagement_type "note", contact_ids ["crm_crmcontact_24b6f46325f0"] (from OE 6), and body summarizing the outstanding $14,000 deposit condition, the three-item supporting-documentation list now requested from the borrower, and the planned Elena-to-Destiny follow-up call on the morning of April 29, 2026 (the day after the fixed universe date of April 28, 2026) -- so the call is logged on Destiny Pham's CRM record before the reminder slips.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Elena's reply confirms that Marketplace listing screenshots and Messenger buyer conversations are the primary supporting evidence she and Sofia identified, while the full three-item supporting-documentation menu -- including the bank-side withdrawal/transfer trail -- is explicitly documented in the underwriter's condition notes discovered in OE 2 and further corroborated by Sofia's escalation email to Carlos in OE 5 (which specifically names "screenshots, buyer messages, withdrawal trail").

—Run #5No justification
✗Run #6The agent never called mortgage_los_update_condition. Because all LOS search calls returned 0 results, the agent could not obtain loan_id or condition_id, and it explicitly noted it could not update the LOS directly. It instead wrote a filesystem text file as a workaround.
